rationale: Threads KOTS env-managed Jira DC service accounts end-to-end. New module `enterprise/integrations/jira_dc/jira_dc_service_account.py` exports `JiraDcServiceAccount` dataclass, `get_jira_dc_service_account_config_error()` validating XOR-presence of `JIRA_DC_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL`/`PAT`, email regex, and no-whitespace; `get_jira_dc_managed_service_account()` returns the env-managed account or None; `resolve_jira_dc_service_account(workspace, token_manager)` prefers env credentials and falls back to decrypting `workspace.svc_acc_api_key`. `server/auth/constants.py` adds the two env constants. `jira_dc_manager.py` replaces every `self.token_manager.decrypt_text(workspace.svc_acc_api_key)` callsite (receive_message, start_job, _add_acknowledgement_reaction, _send_error_comment, _send_repo_selection_comment) with `resolve_jira_dc_service_account(workspace, self.token_manager)` and uses `service_account.email`/`.api_key`. The serialized `_decrypted_api_key` field is dropped from `JiraDcNewConversationView`; `JiraDcV1CallbackProcessor` no longer stores the PAT and instead resolves it at callback time by re-fetching the workspace via `JiraDcIntegrationStore.get_instance().get_workspace_by_name(self.workspace_name)`. The POST route `_resolve_submitted_service_account` substitutes managed credentials when configured and 400s on missing email otherwise; route also short-circuits with a 500 when env config is partially set. Frontend `WebClientConfig` gains `jira_dc_service_account_managed/_email/_config_error` fields; `default_web_client_config_injector.py` derives them from env via `_get_jira_dc_service_account_*` helpers; `jira-dc-integration-panel.tsx` hides the email/PAT inputs and shows a read-only `Managed in Admin Console` block when managed, surfaces `serviceAccountConfigError`, and adjusts `apiKeyRequired`/`isSubmitDisabled`. Three i18n keys are added with translation entries (English-only values for non-English locales — intentional placeholder pending translation). Tests cover env-vs-workspace resolution, partial-config errors, callback-time resolution, and the managed-account route path.
